that the exposition was not only to
commemorate the past, but also to
present the advantages of the pres
ent, and inspire hope for the future
the future of the northwest and
therefore the future of America. Ap-
plause followed the conclusion Of
President Goodies address and the
band struck up patriotic air. amid

Address by Governor.


The next address was made by
Governor George f. Chamberlain on
behalf OF the state OF Oregon. The
speaker, who was warmly greeted.
described In detail the long cherished
design Of Thomas Jefferson and its
fulfillment In the Lewis and Clark ex-
cry. and told how these intrepid ex-
plorers with few heroic followers
guided and directed through the bar
rem waste and over the mountain
wild were oft-times inspired by the
sagacity and loyal devotion of SAGA
yawea. an untutored. Indian bird
woman" and unfurled the Stars and
Stripes at the mouth of the Columbia
on the 9TH day of November, 1905,
and laid the foundation for the ac-
QUESTION by the United States of a
territory equal. II not superior. In
wealth and Importance to the Louis
lana purchase. The governor con-
cluded, amid applause, by welcoming
the world on behalf of the state OF
Oregon to the exposition.


Judge George IL. Williams, mayor
of Portland. formerly attorney gem
eral of the United states. and the
only surviving member of President
Grants cabinet, the grand old man
OF Oregon" received an ovation. In
brief address he welcomed visitors
to the exposition on behalf of the city
OF Portland The speaker dwelt on
the northwest of today as compared
with the northwest of SO years ago
